def solve(nums):
    n = nums.length
    sorted_nums = nums.sort
    
    # 1. Find all divisors k of n
    divisors = []
    (1..Math.sqrt(n)).each do |i|
        if n % i == 0
            divisors << i
            divisors << n / i unless i * i == n
        end
    end
    
    total_sum = 0
    
    # Use a separator that is unlikely to appear naturally in standard integer representations
    separator = "\x01" 
    
    # 2. Check each divisor k
    divisors.sort.each do |k|
        is_sortable = true
        
        # Iterate over blocks starting at j = 0, k, 2k, ...
        (0...n).step(k) do |j|
            # A is the block from nums
            a_block = nums[j, k]
            # S_i is the corresponding block from sorted_nums
            s_block = sorted_nums[j, k]
            
            # Check if multisets match. If they don't, they cannot be cyclic permutations.
            if a_block.sort != s_block.sort
                is_sortable = false
                break
            end
            
            # Check cyclic permutation using string matching.
            a_str = a_block.join(separator)
            s_str = s_block.join(separator)
            s_double_str = s_str + s_str
            
            unless s_double_str.include?(a_str)
                is_sortable = false
                break
            end
        end
        
        if is_sortable
            total_sum += k
        end
    end
    
    return total_sum

    n = nums.length
    
    # 1. Find all divisors k of n
    divisors = []
    i = 1
    while i * i <= n
        if n % i == 0
            divisors << i
            divisors << n / i unless i * i == n
        end
        i += 1
    end
    
    # 2. Sort nums to get S
    s = nums.dup.sort
    
    total_sum = 0
    
    # Helper function to check cyclic permutation
    # Checks if S is a cyclic permutation of A
    is_cyclic = lambda do |A, S|
        k = A.length
        if k == 1
            # If k=1, rotation does nothing, A must equal S
            return A[0] == S[0]
        end
        
        # Check all k possible starting shifts (j) in A
        (0...k).each do |j| 
            match = true
            # Compare S[l] against A rotated by j
            (0...k).each do |l| 
                # A[(j + l) % k] is the element from A that corresponds to S[l]
                if S[l] != A[(j + l) % k]
                    match = false
                    break
                end
            end
            return true if match
        end
        false
    end

    # 3. Check each divisor k
    divisors.each do |k|
        sortable = true
        m = n / k # Number of segments
        
        (0...m).each do |i|
            start_index = i * k
            
            # Extract segments A_i (from nums) and S_i (from sorted s)

                sortable = false
                break
            end
        end
        
        if sortable
            total_sum += k
        end
    end
    
    total_sum

def solve(nums):
    n = nums.length
    s = nums.sort

            divisors << k
        end

        num_blocks = n / k

            s_i = s[start_index..end_index]

        end
    end

def solve(nums)
    n = nums.length
    s = nums.sort
    total_sum = 0
    
    # Find divisors of N
    divisors = []
    i = 1
    while i * i <= n
        if n % i == 0
            divisors << i
            divisors << n / i unless i * i == n
        end
        i += 1
    end
    
    # Helper function to check if S is a cyclic permutation of A
    # Assumes A and S are arrays of integers of the same length k
    check_cyclic = lambda do |A, S|
        k = A.length
        return true if k == 0
        
        # Use a delimiter safe for integer representation
        delimiter = "|||" 
        str_A = A.join(delimiter)
        str_S = S.join(delimiter)
        
        # If S is a cyclic shift of A, S must be a substring of A concatenated with itself
        (str_A + str_A).include?(str_S)
    end

    divisors.each do |k|
        is_sortable = true
        m = n / k # Number of blocks
        
        (0...m).each do |i|
            start_index = i * k
            end_index = (i + 1) * k
            
            # A_i: original segment
            a_i = nums[start_index...end_index]
            # S_i: target segment (from sorted array S)
            s_i = s[start_index...end_index]
            
            # Check if S_i is a cyclic permutation of A_i
            unless check_cyclic.call(a_i, s_i)
                is_sortable = false
                break
            end
        end
        
        if is_sortable
            total_sum += k
        end
    end
    
    total_sum
end
